hero.headline: Clarity. Made visible.
hero.lead: Transad is a minimal marketing and branding agency. We help modern businesses communicate with clarity, consistency, and visual impact.

about.headline: Minimal by intent. Strategic by practice.
about.lede: We design for clarity first — clean identities, strategic campaigns, and refined digital content built around simplicity, purpose, and strong visual direction.
about.body1: Transad works with founders, marketers, and in-house teams who care how their company shows up in the world. Every engagement begins with a question, not a deck.
about.body2: Stuttgart. Leeds. Pristina. Vilnius. Four cities, one studio, one standard of work — across web, social, campaigns, and brand.

services.headline: What we make, and the rules behind it.

identity.title: Clean identities
identity.body: Marks, wordmarks, and the systems that hold them together — built for clarity and consistency across every surface a business shows up on.

campaigns.title: Strategic campaigns
campaigns.body: Launch creative, paid, and editorial campaigns built around a single idea — designed for visual impact, then engineered to scale.

digital.title: Refined digital content
digital.body: Marketing sites, social systems, and presentation kits with the same minimal layouts and strong typography running through every touchpoint.

direction.title: Visual direction
direction.body: Ongoing art direction for in-house teams — keeping the work elegant, professional, and on-brand long after the launch.

contact.headline: Tell us what you're working on.
contact.intro: We take on a small number of projects each year. The shorter and more specific your note, the faster we can reply.

form.projectPlaceholder: A few lines about the project, the timeline, and the budget.
form.disclaimer: We reply within two working days.
form.confirmation: Note received. We'll reply within two working days.
form.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again, or email info@transadgroup.com directly.
form.errorValidation: Please check the fields and try again.

cookies.title: We use cookies
cookies.description: We use only essential cookies by default. With your consent, we may also use analytics or marketing cookies to improve the site and reach the right audience. You can change your choice at any time.

categories.necessaryDescription: Required for the site to function — language preference, security, and consent state. Cannot be disabled.
categories.analyticsDescription: Help us understand how visitors use the site, in aggregate. No cross-site tracking.
categories.marketingDescription: Used to measure campaign performance and serve relevant content. No data sold to third parties.

sections.title: EU dispute resolution
paragraphs: The European Commission provides a platform for online dispute resolution (OS): https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr/. You can find our email address in the contact section above.

sections.title: Consumer dispute resolution
paragraphs: We are not willing or obliged to participate in dispute resolution proceedings before a consumer arbitration board.

sections.title: Liability for content
paragraphs: As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages in accordance with Section 7 (1) TMG. According to Sections 8 to 10 TMG, however, we are not obliged to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information or to investigate circumstances that indicate illegal activity.
Obligations to remove or block the use of information under general laws remain unaffected. Liability in this respect is only possible from the time of knowledge of a specific infringement.

sections.title: Liability for links
paragraphs: Our website contains links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no influence. Therefore, we cannot accept any liability for this third-party content.

The respective provider or operator of the linked pages is always responsible for the content. Linked pages were checked for possible legal violations at the time of linking. Illegal content was not recognisable at the time of linking.

sections.title: Copyright
paragraphs: The content and works on these pages created by the site operators are subject to German copyright law. Duplication, processing, distribution, and any kind of exploitation outside the limits of copyright law require the written consent of the respective author or creator.

Insofar as the content on this site was not created by the operator, the copyrights of third parties are observed. Should you nevertheless become aware of a copyright infringement, please inform us. Upon notification of violations, we will remove such content immediately.

privacy.headline: Privacy policy
privacy.lead: How we handle personal data on this website. Compliant with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

sections.title: Controller
paragraphs:
Controller within the meaning of Art. 4 (7) GDPR is:
[Company Name]
[Address]
Email: info@transadgroup.com

sections.title: Scope
paragraphs: This privacy policy applies to all data we process when you visit transad.studio or interact with us through this site. It does not cover services operated by third parties that we link to but do not control.

sections.title: What we collect and why
paragraphs: We process two categories of personal data: data you actively provide (e.g. via the contact form) and data automatically transmitted by your browser when you load a page (e.g. IP address, user agent, request timestamp).

sections.title: Legal basis
paragraphs: We process data on the following legal bases under Art. 6 (1) GDPR: with your consent (lit. a), to perform a contract or take pre-contractual steps (lit. b), to comply with legal obligations (lit. c), and to safeguard our legitimate interests in operating a secure, functional website (lit. f).

sections.title: Cookies
paragraphs: This site does not set marketing or analytics cookies. Strictly necessary cookies may be used to remember your language preference. No consent banner is required for these technical cookies.

sections.title: Contact form
paragraphs: When you send us a message via the contact form, we receive your name, email address, optional company name, and the contents of your message. We use this data solely to respond to your enquiry. The data is stored for as long as the resulting business relationship requires, plus any statutory retention periods.

sections.title: Hosting and server logs
paragraphs: This site is hosted by [Hosting Provider]. The hosting provider processes log files containing your IP address, browser type, request URL, and timestamp. Logs are kept for [N] days for security and operational purposes (Art. 6 (1) lit. f GDPR).

sections.title: Analytics
paragraphs: [We do not use analytics tools.] / [We use [Analytics Provider] with IP anonymisation and without cookies. The data is processed on the basis of Art. 6 (1) lit. f GDPR — our legitimate interest in understanding aggregate site usage.]

sections.title: Third-party services
paragraphs: We use [Email Service Provider — e.g. Resend] to deliver emails sent through the contact form. Personal data is transmitted only insofar as necessary to deliver the message.

sections.title: Data retention
paragraphs: We retain personal data only as long as necessary for the stated purposes or as required by law. Once the purpose is fulfilled, the data is deleted unless statutory retention periods (e.g. tax law) apply.

sections.title: Your rights
paragraphs: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights regarding your personal data:
list: Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR)
Right to withdraw consent at any time (Art. 7 (3) GDPR)

sections.title: Right to lodge a complaint
paragraphs: You have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority — typically the data-protection authority of your federal state, or in our case the [Landesbeauftragte für Datenschutz und Informationsfreiheit Nordrhein-Westfalen].

sections.title: Changes to this policy
paragraphs: We may update this privacy policy from time to time to reflect legal or operational changes. The current version always governs and is dated above.

terms.headline: Terms of service
terms.lead: General terms governing engagements between [Company Name] and our clients.

sections.title: Scope
paragraphs: These terms (the "Terms") govern all engagements between [Company Name] ("we", "us") and our clients ("you", "Client") for design, branding, and marketing services. Conflicting client terms are not accepted unless explicitly agreed in writing.

sections.title: Services
paragraphs: The scope of services for each engagement is defined in the relevant proposal, statement of work, or order confirmation ("SOW"). Where the SOW and these Terms conflict, the SOW prevails.

sections.title: Conclusion of contract
paragraphs: A contract is concluded when you accept our written proposal in writing (including by email) or when we begin performing services with your express agreement.

sections.title: Pricing and payment
paragraphs: Fees, milestones, and payment terms are defined in the SOW. Unless otherwise agreed, invoices are due within 14 days of receipt, net, without deduction. We reserve the right to charge interest on overdue amounts at the statutory rate.

sections.title: Cooperation
paragraphs: Timely delivery depends on your cooperation. You agree to provide briefs, materials, approvals, and feedback within the timelines defined in the SOW. Delays on your side may shift project timelines accordingly.

sections.title: Delivery
paragraphs: Delivery dates given without an explicit binding commitment are estimates. Binding deadlines must be agreed in writing in the SOW.

sections.title: Intellectual property and rights of use
paragraphs: Upon full payment of all fees due under the relevant SOW, you receive the rights of use to the agreed deliverables for the scope and territory specified in the SOW. Working files, source materials, and unselected concepts remain our property unless explicitly transferred.
We retain the right to reference completed work in our portfolio, case studies, and marketing — unless otherwise agreed in writing.

sections.title: Confidentiality
paragraphs: Each party will treat confidential information received from the other party as confidential and will not disclose it to third parties without prior written consent, except as required by law.

sections.title: Termination
paragraphs: Engagements may be terminated for cause at any time. Termination for convenience is governed by the SOW; in the absence of a specific clause, either party may terminate with [N] weeks' written notice. Fees for work performed up to the termination date remain payable.

sections.title: Liability
paragraphs: We are liable without limitation for damages caused by intent or gross negligence. For damages caused by simple negligence, our liability is limited to typical, foreseeable damages and capped at the total fees paid under the relevant SOW. Liability under the German Product Liability Act (Produkthaftungsgesetz) and for injury to life, body, or health remains unaffected.

sections.title: Data protection
paragraphs: Both parties comply with applicable data protection laws. Personal data processing on the agency side is described in our privacy policy.

sections.title: Final provisions
paragraphs: German law applies, excluding the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. Place of jurisdiction is [City], to the extent permitted by law. Should any provision of these Terms be invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain in full force and effect.

hello-baby-box.title: Hello Baby Box
hello-baby-box.outcome: A modern direct-to-consumer baby brand built from a single Shopify store into a 5× revenue business.

hero.alt: A mother holding a newborn wrapped in a pink Hello Baby Box wrap, with the brand badge visible.

brief.body: Hello Baby Box came to us as a young direct-to-consumer baby brand with a strong product but no clear digital identity, a basic Shopify store, and inconsistent marketing. The founders needed a partner who could turn the brand into a recognizable presence — not just run ads, but build the entire commercial engine around it.

approach.body: We started with the Shopify experience: a quieter, more confident store design that lets product photography and parent-focused copy carry the brand. We then layered on a sustained content program — monthly editorial photography, a recognizable social grid, and performance creative that runs continuously across Meta. Every asset reinforces the same calm, considered tone.

showcase.alt: Hello Baby Box homepage shown on a laptop in a soft natural-lit setting.
showcase.caption: Shopify storefront — redesigned 2023

showcase.alt: Hello Baby Box mobile product listing page on iPhone.
showcase.caption: Mobile commerce — 70% of traffic

showcase.alt: Three Hello Baby Box social media posts in sequence.
showcase.caption: Social content — monthly editorial calendar

results.headline: From a single Shopify store to a 5× revenue business in three years.
bullets: 5× revenue growth between 2023 and 2025
Continuous performance creative across Meta (Facebook + Instagram)
A recognizable social presence with consistent monthly editorial output
A retained partnership now entering its fourth year

treventi.title: Treventi Doors
treventi.outcome: From a regional door retailer to a recognized local manufacturer, built on three years of consistent brand presence.

meta.label: Services
meta.value: Brand Awareness, Website, Social, Campaigns, Engagement

hero.alt: The Treventi website on a laptop showing the brand tagline 'Crafting Quality. Building Possibilities.' over a craftsman at work.

brief.body: Treventi began as a regional retailer of imported interior doors in Kosovo, with strong product knowledge but limited brand recognition outside their immediate market. As the founders prepared to transition into local manufacturing, they needed a brand presence that matched the ambition — one that positioned Treventi as a quality-led name people would actively choose, not just discover at point of sale.

approach.body: We rebuilt the brand around the idea of craftsmanship — anchoring every piece of communication in the people, process, and product behind the doors. A new website foregrounds the manufacturing story with editorial photography and a calm, considered tone. A continuous social program runs across Facebook and Instagram with high-touch creative. Performance campaigns drive awareness across Kosovo's major cities, supported by retargeting and engagement-led content.

showcase.alt: A map of Kosovo showing Treventi's growth from a single Pristina location to a network across Prizren, Peja, Drenas, Ferizaj and a manufacturing facility.
showcase.caption: Growth across Kosovo — from one store to a local manufacturer

showcase.alt: Treventi homepage hero on desktop with the manufacturing story.
showcase.caption: Website — desktop experience

showcase.alt: Editorial section of the Treventi website showing the door manufacturing process.
showcase.caption: Production story — editorial content

showcase.alt: Treventi mobile experience — homepage, founders page, and red navigation menu.
showcase.caption: Mobile experience — responsive from launch

showcase.alt: A grid of Treventi social media posts in red and white, mixing lifestyle photography with product imagery.
showcase.caption: Social content — continuous editorial output

showcase.alt: Three Facebook ad creatives for Treventi shown on iPhones.
showcase.caption: Performance campaigns — Meta ads across Kosovo

results.headline: A regional retailer repositioned as a recognized local manufacturer.
bullets: Three-year retained partnership across brand, content, and performance
Brand presence extended from a single city to six across Kosovo
Successful narrative shift from retailer to local manufacturer
Continuous social and ad creative output sustained for three years

stem-skincare.title: Stem Skincare
stem-skincare.outcome: Lifted ROAS from 4× to 9.5× while scaling ad spend 17× — more than double the industry benchmark for beauty e-commerce.

hero.alt: A close-up portrait of a woman with the Stem Skincare brand pillars overlaid as three intersecting circles: pastro, trajto, hidrato (cleanse, treat, hydrate).

brief.body: Stem Skincare came to us a month into launch, after their previous agency had failed to translate a strong product into commercial traction. Spend was sitting around €400–500 a month, generating roughly €2,000 in sales — ROAS that wouldn't sustain a growing brand. They needed a partner who could rebuild the entire commercial engine: positioning, creative, media, and the website experience underneath it all.

approach.body: We started by tightening the visual language — clean editorial photography, calm typography, ingredient-led storytelling — and rebuilt the social presence around real skin, real ingredients, and a confident product narrative. From there we restructured the ad account: Advantage+ shopping campaigns, dedicated launch creative, and continuous performance creative rotation across Meta. Every euro of spend was tracked against revenue, and creative was iterated weekly.

showcase.alt: Three Stem Skincare Vitamin C 15% serum boxes photographed in soft natural light against a clean white background.
showcase.caption: Packaging — Vitamin C 15% serum range

showcase.alt: Editorial flatlay of the Stem Skincare Vitamin C serum bottle on a stone tray with sliced lemon and dropper.
showcase.caption: Editorial product photography

showcase.alt: Three Instagram carousel slides showing close-up skin photography with ingredient overlays in Albanian.
showcase.caption: Social content — ingredient-led storytelling

showcase.alt: Three Instagram posts showing UVA/UVB protection education, gift card promotion, and dual ingredient combinations.
showcase.caption: Social campaigns — education & promotion

results.headline: From €2k a month in sales to a 9.5× ROAS in under a year.
bullets: 9.5× return on ad spend across a 10-month period (Apr 2024 – Feb 2025)
€81,772 in tracked website revenue from €8,584 in ad spend
2,779 website purchases attributed to Meta campaigns
66,000+ link clicks driven across Facebook and Instagram
Continuous creative rotation: launch campaigns, ingredient education, gift-card promotions, and seasonal pushes

aqh.eyebrow: Work / 04 — Campaign · OOH · Art Direction · Public Health
aqh.title: AQH — Accessible Quality Healthcare
aqh.outcome: A nationwide public health campaign on smoking, nutrition, and cardiovascular care — placed across underground stations, bus shelters, and street-level OOH.

meta.label: Sector
meta.value: SDC project implemented by Swiss TPH

hero.alt: A large underground station billboard for AQH showing a young boy wearing an oxygen mask, with the headline 'Stop smoking, protect the health of your loved ones.'

brief.body: AQH is a Swiss-funded public health initiative — an SDC project implemented by Swiss TPH — focused on improving access to quality healthcare and prevention. The brief was to develop a nationwide awareness campaign across multiple public health themes: secondhand smoke, cardiovascular disease, hypertension, and family wellbeing, executed across high-impact OOH formats and supporting print.

approach.body: We approached this as serious editorial work, not soft NGO communication. The campaign uses arresting photography and direct, single-sentence headlines that respect the audience's intelligence. A flat, bold colour system — derived from a set of pillar icons covering different aspects of health — gives each message a distinct identity while keeping the overall campaign coherent. Across underground billboards, bus shelters, building-side posters, and pocket print, the visual logic stays consistent: one image, one message, one colour cue.

showcase.alt: AQH underground station billboard photographed in situ — a child in an oxygen mask with the headline 'Stop smoking, protect the health of your loved ones.'
showcase.caption: Underground billboard — stop smoking campaign

showcase.alt: AQH bus shelter poster at night — the same secondhand smoke campaign placed at street level.
showcase.caption: Bus shelter — night placement

showcase.alt: Two paired campaign posters — one with the boy in the oxygen mask, one in solid red with the headline 'Take care of your family.'
showcase.caption: Paired posters — image & type executions

showcase.alt: A street-level building billboard showing a grandfather and grandchild gardening together, with the headline 'Eating healthy and spending time with your loved ones helps hypertension.'
showcase.caption: Hypertension campaign — street placement

showcase.alt: Multiple AQH campaign posters in red, purple, blue and green — each addressing a different health theme.
showcase.caption: Colour-coded campaign system across health pillars

showcase.alt: Eight coloured circular icons representing the AQH health pillars — heart rate, heart, walking, cycling, strength, nutrition, swimming, hiking.
showcase.caption: Health pillar icon system

results.headline: A nationwide public health campaign placed across high-traffic OOH formats.
bullets: Multi-theme campaign covering smoking, hypertension, and cardiovascular health
Executed across underground billboards, bus shelters, building-side posters, and supporting print
Unified by a colour-coded pillar system that links each message to its health theme
Delivered for AQH, an SDC-funded project implemented by Swiss TPH

asllani-dental.title: Asllani Family Dental Clinic
asllani-dental.outcome: An identity for a family-run dental practice — built around warmth, trust, and the quiet confidence patients want from their clinic.

hero.alt: A close-up portrait of a smiling young girl with freckles, with the Asllani Family Dental Clinic logo lockup applied in the upper corner — showing the brand's warmth in a real lifestyle context.

brief.body: Asllani Family Dental Clinic is a multi-generational dental practice run by Drs. Jakup, Arta, and Agon Asllani. They needed an identity that reflected the reality of their work — that a dental visit is rarely just about treatment. For most patients, especially families and children, it's about feeling safe, cared for, and understood. The previous identity didn't communicate that. The new one had to.

approach.body: We designed the mark as two interlocking forms held together as one — a continuous, symmetrical shape that reads as a soft seal of care rather than a clinical icon. The geometry is precise but the silhouette stays gentle, avoiding the cold feel that dental branding so often falls into. The wordmark sits in a humanist sans-serif — open, friendly, easy to read across signage, social, and patient-facing materials. The palette is built around a calm, restorative blue that feels reassuring rather than sterile. The system flexes from large-scale architectural signage down to small social applications without losing its character.

pillars: Trust — a mark that reads professional first, friendly always
Warmth — designed for the people most nervous about the dentist
Longevity — a system built to flex from clinic signage to social

showcase.alt: The Asllani Family Dental Clinic primary logo lockup in white on the brand's signature soft blue.
showcase.caption: Logo on brand blue

showcase.alt: The same logo lockup rendered in soft blue on a clean white ground — the inverse colorway used for light surfaces.
showcase.caption: Inverse — for light surfaces

showcase.alt: Brand applied in a real lifestyle context — a close-up portrait of a smiling young woman with freckles, with the Asllani Family Dental Clinic logo lockup placed in the upper area of the frame.
showcase.caption: Brand applied in context — lifestyle portrait

showcase.alt: The Asllani Family Dental Clinic logo rendered as white architectural signage on the dark fascia of a modern building, with glass facade below.
showcase.caption: Architectural signage — building application

results.headline: A dental identity that puts the patient before the procedure.
bullets: A custom symbol formed of two interlocking shapes — a continuous seal of care rather than a clinical icon
Humanist wordmark designed for clarity at every scale
A calm, restorative blue palette chosen to reassure rather than clinical-white
A flexible system designed to live across signage, print, social, and digital
Built for a multi-generational family practice — Drs. Jakup, Arta, and Agon Asllani

floovle.eyebrow: Work / 06 — Identity · Brand Strategy · Campaign · Digital Platform
floovle.title: Floovle
floovle.outcome: A new digital platform for the global aesthetic medicine industry — built on clarity, trust, and emotional intelligence in a category long defined by noise.

meta.label: Services
meta.value: Brand Identity, Strategy, Campaign, Platform Design, Art Direction

hero.alt: The Floovle wordmark set in a luminous pink aura — a soft glowing gradient from deep magenta to lighter rose, evoking warmth, focus, and clarity.

brief.body: Floovle was founded in 2024 to address a problem the aesthetic medicine industry has long avoided: the absence of a trustworthy, emotionally intelligent space where people can make informed decisions about their bodies. The category is loud, commercial, and often deliberately confusing. Floovle came to us pre-launch with a clear vision — Clarity, Trust, Empowerment — but no identity, no brand language, no campaign. The brief was to build all of it.

approach.body: We anchored the brand around a single visual idea: the aura. A soft, breathing gradient of pink and magenta that surrounds — never overwhelms — the people, the type, and the platform itself. It's a frame for focus inside infinity. The wordmark is open, rounded, and intentionally calm; the typography system reads more like a wellness app than a medical product. The campaign treats the audience as adults — direct propositions like 'Synonym for trusted aesthetic decisions' and 'Connect with beauty experts' replace the usual before-and-after marketing. Across OOH, social, the digital platform, and brand collateral, every touchpoint reinforces the same promise: order brought to an overwhelming category.

pillars: Clarity — a frame for focus inside infinity
Trust — vetted providers, verified content, no noise
Empowerment — from curiosity to confident decision

showcase.alt: The Floovle wordmark centered inside a luminous pink-to-magenta aura, the brand's signature visual device.
showcase.caption: Primary mark — the aura system

showcase.alt: A white t-shirt printed with the Floovle aura — a soft pink glow surrounding the wordmark — worn by a person in soft daylight.
showcase.caption: Brand applied — apparel

showcase.alt: The Floovle business card system — a pink gradient card paired with a clean white reverse showing contact details and a QR code.
showcase.caption: Stationery — two-sided system

showcase.alt: A campaign card in soft lavender with the Floovle headline 'Connect with beauty experts' and the supporting line 'Made for you.'
showcase.caption: Campaign — connect with beauty experts

showcase.alt: The Floovle 'Discover. Learn. Connect.' subway poster placed inside a metro car — a soft-focus portrait of a young woman framed by hands.
showcase.caption: Subway OOH — discover. learn. connect.

showcase.alt: A street-level billboard with the Floovle headline 'Synonym for trusted aesthetic decisions' overlaid on a confident close-up portrait.
showcase.caption: Street billboard — trusted aesthetic decisions

showcase.alt: The Floovle digital platform shown across multiple desktop screens — doctor profiles, search and filter, an editorial blog, account settings, and a detailed practitioner page.
showcase.caption: Digital platform — doctor discovery, editorial, accounts

results.headline: Building the global standard for trusted aesthetic guidance.
bullets: Full brand identity built around a single recognizable visual device — the aura
Campaign system spanning OOH, subway, social, and digital, unified by tone and palette
Digital platform design covering doctor discovery, editorial content, search, and account flows
Strategic positioning anchored in three core values: Clarity, Trust, Empowerment
A category-redefining brand prepared for global scale, designed to feel emotionally resonant rather than commercial

servicesPage.headline: Four disciplines, one direction.
servicesPage.lead: Each engagement combines four disciplines. The mix shifts with the brief; the standard does not.

social.title: Social media marketing
social.headline: Show up consistently. Sound like you.
social.body: We run channels as editorial systems, not feeds. Content is planned in calendars, produced on schedule, approved monthly, and tuned by the conversation it generates.
deliverables: Channel management across Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, TikTok
Editorial calendars with monthly approval cycles
Original content production for social and web

content.title: Content creation & production
content.headline: Make the work, then make it move.
content.body: Photography, copy, layout, and motion produced inside one studio. The result is consistent across surfaces because it was made by the same hands.
deliverables: Video editing, subtitles, and motion graphics
Photo direction, retouching, and stock curation

advertising.title: Online advertising
advertising.headline: Spend deliberately. Measure honestly.
advertising.body: Paid media run by the people who built the creative. We test, rotate, and report against the metrics that move your business — not vanity dashboards.
deliverables: Performance creative rotation and A/B testing

strategy.title: Strategy & reporting
strategy.headline: A monthly cadence, not a quarterly review.
strategy.body: Strategy calls, KPI dashboards, and weekly reports built around the work — so the next decision is obvious. We don't manage relationships from a distance.
deliverables: Monthly strategy calls and quarterly reviews
Performance reports — weekly or monthly cadence
Live KPI dashboards tailored to the brief

pricing.headline: Three packages, one outcome.
pricing.lead: Pick the entry point that matches where you are. Each package scales with your growth — no rebuilds, no surprises.

starter.title: Build presence.
starter.description: The entry point into professional digital marketing. We set up your social media presence, produce content consistently, and run your first targeted ads — so your audience finds you.
starter.idealFor: SMEs with fewer than 20 employees, local service providers, and companies just starting out with social media.

categories.title: Social media marketing
items: 12 pieces of content for social media and the web

categories.title: Content creation
items: Stock photo curation from premium libraries

categories.title: Online advertising
